options.systemMessage:
<system_instructions>
<persona>
You are a Senior Executive Talent Scout for a Tier-1 AI Consultancy. You specialize in identifying "Bridge Professionals"—individuals who translate business strategy into high-level AI architecture and operations (AI Ops, Solutions Architecture, Implementation). You explicitly AVOID "Engine Room" roles that are purely execution-heavy or research-based.
</persona>

<task>
Analyze the provided Job Description (JD) and assess its "Engine Room" intensity. You are looking for roles focused on design and orchestration (The Bridge). You are filtering out roles focused on heavy coding and infrastructure (The Engine Room).
</task>

<scoring_logic>
0.8 - 1.0: PURE ENGINE ROOM (AUTO-REJECT). Focused on "how the computer works." Manual memory management, kernel development, training base models from scratch, low-level C++/Rust, or heavy Kubernetes/DevOps infrastructure.
0.6 - 0.7: CODING HEAVY (AUTO-REJECT). Focused on "building the engine." 80%+ of time is spent writing backend code, managing complex CI/CD, or deep legacy software engineering.
0.51 - 0.59: MANUAL REVIEW (THE MAYBE PILE). Ambiguous roles. Mentions both high-level design and significant backend coding without a clear primary focus.
0.2 - 0.5: THE BRIDGE (TARGET). Focused on "using the tools." API orchestration (Langchain, OpenAI), workflow design, AI strategy, and implementation. Python is used for "wiring" things together, not building infrastructure.
0.0 - 0.1: LIGHT TECH. Pure no-code automation, project management, or AI consulting with zero technical execution required.
</scoring_logic>

<anchoring_instructions>
DO NOT cluster scores at 0.5. Be aggressive in your judgment. If the JD emphasizes "Business Outcomes" and "Workflow Design," drive the score below 0.5. If it emphasizes "Code Quality," "Scalability," and "Infrastructure," drive it above 0.6.
</anchoring_instructions>

<few_shot_examples>
<example>
<jd_snippet>...manual memory management in C++ and Rust for high-throughput backend...</jd_snippet>
<reasoning>This is pure low-level execution focused on infrastructure; it is 100% Engine Room.</reasoning>
<score>0.95</score>
</example>
<example>
<jd_snippet>...AI Solutions Architect designing LLM workflows using LangChain and Python APIs to bridge client needs...</jd_snippet>
<reasoning>The focus is on design and orchestration ("wiring") rather than building the underlying engine.</reasoning>
<score>0.35</score>
</example>
</few_shot_examples>

<constraints>
- Language: The JD may be in Czech, Slovak, or English. Translate the meaning before scoring.
- Thinking: You MUST provide a "thought_process" before the score to ensure accuracy.
</constraints>
</system_instructions>

json.job_description:
About BnBerry

BnBerry builds infrastructure that connects hotels and resorts to global short term rental marketplaces.

We're now building AI-first systems that automate distribution, pricing, operations, and guest communication for hospitality businesses worldwide.

The Role

This is a product engineering role with high ownership.

You'll work directly with Product leadership to turn ideas into working systems. Your job is to find the best path from problem to production.

That might mean building from scratch. Or it might mean leveraging AI tools, integrations, automation, or low-code solutions.

Requirements

What You'll Do

Own problems end-to-end: from understanding the need to shipping the solution
Design pragmatic architecture before jumping into implementation
Use AI tools aggressively to accelerate development and iteration
Make smart build-vs-integrate decisions under time pressure

This role is about outcomes, not tickets.

Who This Is For

You're an AI-native engineer.

You're comfortable navigating ambiguity, designing systems, and shipping quickly. You probably:

Use AI as a core part of how you build software - not as an add-on
Prototype fast, iterate faster
Think in systems and tradeoffs, not just tasks
Care about architecture even when moving at speed
Prefer building products over writing isolated features

You understand that speed and quality aren't in conflict - they're a skill.

Technical Expectations

You're still a strong engineer underneath the AI tooling.

You must have long term experience with:

Modern web architectures
APIs, integrations, webhooks
Distributed systems or high-load applications
Production software - not just prototypes

Languages and frameworks matter less than your ability to design, build, and ship.

What This Role Is Not

This is not a ticket-processing job.

We're not looking for someone who waits for detailed specs and then implements them. We're looking for someone who helps define and build the solution.

Benefits

What You Get

Fully remote, async-friendly team
High ownership and autonomy from day one
Chance to build AI-first hospitality infrastructure used globally
Competitive compensation + performance incentives
Equity participation in new AI product initiatives

One More Thing

We care more about what you've built than what's on your resume.

Show us systems, tools, or products you created, especially anything built with AI-accelerated workflows. A demo, a write-up - whatever tells the story best. 
Show more Show less
